title: Create your first functional component
description: Create a Greeting component that accepts a name prop and displays 'Hello, {name}!'
explanation: Functional components are JavaScript functions that return JSX. They receive props as an argument and can use destructuring to extract specific props.

title: Build a counter with useState
description: Create a Counter component with increment, decrement, and reset buttons using useState.
hints: Use functional updates for state based on previous state
explanation: useState is the most fundamental React Hook. It returns a stateful value and a function to update it. Using a function updater (c => c + 1) ensures you always have the latest state value.

title: Fetch data with useEffect
description: Create a component that fetches and displays a list of users from an API on mount.
hints: Empty [] dependency array means run once on mount
Return cleanup function to prevent state updates on unmounted component
explanation: useEffect handles side effects (data fetching, subscriptions). The dependency array controls when it reruns. Always cleanup to prevent memory leaks.

title: Create a useFetch custom hook
description: Extract the data fetching logic into a reusable useFetch custom hook.
hints: They can use other hooks internally
explanation: Custom hooks encapsulate reusable stateful logic. They follow the Rules of Hooks and can call other hooks. They don't share state between components — each call creates independent state.

title: Implement a theme context
description: Create a ThemeContext that provides dark/light mode to deeply nested components.
explanation: Context provides a way to pass data through the component tree without prop drilling. Combine it with a custom hook for a clean API.

title: Shopping cart with useReducer
description: Implement a shopping cart using useReducer with ADD_ITEM, REMOVE_ITEM, and CLEAR_CART actions.
hints: useReducer is useful for complex state logic
Actions describe what happened, reducers define how state changes
explanation: useReducer is an alternative to useState for complex state. The reducer is a pure function that takes current state and an action, returning the new state. It follows the Redux pattern.

title: Optimize with useMemo and useCallback
description: Optimize a slow component using useMemo for expensive calculations and useCallback for stable function references.
hints: memo prevents re-render if props haven't changed
explanation: useMemo memoizes expensive calculations. useCallback memoizes function references. React.memo skips re-rendering if props are the same. Use these when profiling shows performance issues.

title: Create an Error Boundary component
description: Create a class-based ErrorBoundary component that catches rendering errors and shows a fallback UI.
hints: Error boundaries must be class components
explanation: Error Boundaries catch JavaScript errors in child component tree. They must be class components. Use react-error-boundary library for a functional hook-based API.

title: Setup client-side routing
description: Create a multi-page app with React Router v6 with Home, About, and a dynamic User/:id page.
starterCode:
import { BrowserRouter, Routes, Route, Link, useParams } from 'react-router-dom';

// Create components and routes
explanation: React Router v6 uses a declarative Routes/Route pattern. useParams extracts dynamic segments, useNavigate for programmatic navigation.

title: Controlled form with validation
description: Build a login form with email and password fields, validation, and submission handling.
hints: Controlled inputs sync state with DOM
Validate on submit, clear errors on change
explanation: Controlled components keep form data in React state. Every keystroke updates state via onChange. This gives you full control for validation and transformation.
